name: auto-color
description: Workspace-tinted UI: pastel title, activity, and status bars plus a subtle hue across the workbench—golden-ratio palettes, native-style depth, zero setup.

commands.title: Auto Color: Enable for this workspace
Auto Color: Disable for this workspace
Auto Color: Reset to default color

auto-color.enabled.markdownDescription: Same as **Auto Color: Enable (all windows)** / **Disable (all windows)**. When off, no window gets Auto Color until you turn it on again.
auto-color.enabled.description: Global on/off for Auto Color in every window. Same as Enable/Disable (all windows) commands.

enumDescriptions: Title, activity, and status bars plus the tinted editor, sidebar, tabs, panel, and related UI.
Only the title and status bars. Activity bar and the rest of the workbench follow your color theme.
auto-color.scope.markdownDescription: Same as **Auto Color: Set color scope**. Use the Workspace tab to set this per folder.
auto-color.scope.description: Which parts of the UI Auto Color paints. Same as the Set color scope command.

auto-color.workspaceDisabled.markdownDescription: Same as **Auto Color: Disable for this workspace** / **Enable for this workspace**. When enabled, this folder’s workspace skips Auto Color even if the global switch is on.
auto-color.workspaceDisabled.description: Turn Auto Color off for this workspace only. Same as per-workspace enable/disable commands.

auto-color.randomSeed.markdownDescription: Non-zero locks the palette to a random choice (set by **Randomize color**). Use `0` or clear for folder-path colors (**Reset to default color**).
auto-color.randomSeed.description: Random palette seed; 0 means derive from folder path. Set by Randomize / Reset commands.
